It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />

It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />

'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 鳥居（陸側）.JPG | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=en&pagename=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&params=34_22_45.5_N_129_18_42.7_E_region:JP-42_scale:20000&title=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE | deity = [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotamabime]] }}
'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />

{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 鳥居（陸側）.JPG | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=en&pagename=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&params=34_22_45.5_N_129_18_42.7_E_region:JP-42_scale:20000&title=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE | deity = [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotamabime]] }}
'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Nagasaki}}
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 鳥居（陸側）.JPG | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=en&pagename=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&params=34_22_45.5_N_129_18_42.7_E_region:JP-42_scale:20000&title=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE | deity = [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotamabime]] }}
'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsushima, Nagasaki}}
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 鳥居（陸側）.JPG | coordinates = https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=en&pagename=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&params=34_22_45.5_N_129_18_42.7_E_region:JP-42_scale:20000&title=%E5%92%8C%E5%A4%9A%E9%83%BD%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE | deity = [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotamabime]] }}
'''Watazumi Shrine''', also known as '''Watatsumi Shrine''' is a shinto shrine in [[Tsushima, Nagasaki]].<ref name="JapanTimes" />
It is a major tourist destination for tourists from [[South Korea]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /><ref name="Wen-2023" /><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020" /><ref name="Alyse-2023" />[[File:海に臨む鳥居.JPG|thumb| Torii facing the sea]] [[File:Watatsumi.torii.jpg|thumb| Torii seen from the sea side]] [[File:海神神社3.jpg|thumb| Three pillar torii in the pond]] [[File:和多都美神社2.jpg|thumb| Three-pillar torii gate next to the shrine]]
It has a famous row of five torii in a row with two in the ocean similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref name="JapanTimes">{{Cite web |date=2013-09-28 |title=Tsushima: a boundary island of Japan {{!}} The Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130928050316/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/life/2013/09/21/travel/tsushima-a-boundary-island-of-japan/ |archive-date=2013-09-28 }}</ref>
In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-14 |title=Japan's Watatsumi Shrine bans foreigners after vandalism and alleged death threat against priest {{!}} South China Morning Post |url=https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |access-date=2023-11-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114080417/https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/east-asia/article/3046010/japans-watatsumi-shrine-bans-foreigners-after-vandalism-and |archive-date=2020-01-14 }}</ref><ref name="Wen-2023">{{Cite news |last=Wen |first=Lok Jian |date=2023-07-10 |title=Vandalism, peeling ceiling art: Japan temples suffer damage |language=en |work=The Straits Times |url=https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/vandalism-peeling-ceiling-art-japan-temples-suffer-damage-to-tourist-sites#:~:text=In%20western%20Japan's%20Nagasaki%20prefecture,language%20and%20theft%20of%20amulets. |access-date=2023-11-20 |issn=0585-3923}}</ref><ref name="SoraNews24 -Japan News--2020">{{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Japanese shrine bans foreign visitors following disrespectful behaviour by tourists |url=https://soranews24.com/2020/01/13/japanese-shrine-bans-foreign-visitors-following-disrespectful-behaviour-by-tourists/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=SoraNews24 -Japan News- |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Alyse-2023">{{Cite web |last=Alyse |date=2023-02-09 |title=Overtourism in Japan: A Victim of its Own Success? |url=https://www.theinvisibletourist.com/overtourism-in-japan-tourist-pollution/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=The Invisible Tourist |language=en-AU}}</ref>
In 2020 it was damaged by a typhoon and [[Ghost of Tsushima]] fans helped raise money to repair it.<ref name="vgc-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans have helped raise $260k for repairs on the real island |url=https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-have-helped-raise-260k-for-repairs-on-the-real-island/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=VGC |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="destructoid-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-12 |title=Ghost of Tsushima fans come together to help fund repair of Watatsumi Shrine gate |url=https://www.destructoid.com/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-come-together-to-help-fund-repair-of-watatsumi-shrine-gate/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Destructoid |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a">{{Cite web |last=Oloman |first=Jordan |date=2021-01-11 |title=Ghost of Tsushima Fans Help With Real-Life Tsushima Island Repairs |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/ghost-of-tsushima-fans-help-with-real-life-tsushima-island-repairs |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kaijin Shrine Tathagata Buddha |url=https://research.cgu.edu/cultural-property-disputes-resource/cpdr/kaijin-shrine-tathagata-buddha |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Cultural Property Disputes Resource |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Tourist issues == In January 2020 they banned foreigners due to behavioral issues by [[South Korea|South Korean]] guests.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" /> The ban was due to vandalism, thefts, and a threat against the head priest.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
Complaints included picnics at the shrine, a YouTuber filming there without permission, tour guides disrespecting sacred areas, and Korean graffiti. Some amulets were stolen. A Korean tour guide threatened the priest's life.<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
This ban substantially reduced the amount of Korean tourists who visited [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]].<ref name="webarchiveorg-2020a" />
== Typhoon destruction == A typhoon damaged the Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine in Tsushima, Japan. This happened in September 2020.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /><ref name="siliconera-2021a" /> A crowdfunding campaign started on November 27, 2020 on the Japanese website Camp-Fire.<ref name="destructoid-2021a" /> It aimed to repair the gate.<ref name="siliconera-2021a">{{Cite web |date=2021-01-11 |title=Tsushima's Watatsumi Shrine Crowdfunding Ended at Over 500% |url=https://www.siliconera.com/tsushimas-watatsumi-shrine-crowdfunding-ended-at-over-500/ |access-date=2023-11-20 |website=Siliconera |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="oloman-2021a" /><ref name="destructoid-2021a" />
The campaign reached its initial goal quickly. This goal was 5 million yen. It was reached by December 1, 2020. The campaign ended on January 10, 2021. It raised 27,103,882 yen. This is about $260,435. Many donors were fans of the "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]" game.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" />
The restoration was planned to start in April 2021,, and to finish by August 2021. There were plans for a stone monument. It would list the names of those who donated at least 10,000 yen. Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />

Concerns were raised over coronavirus affecting the construction.<ref name="siliconera-2021a" /><ref name="oloman-2021a" />
== In popular culture == In the game "[[Ghost of Tsushima]]," there is a similar shrine. It is the Scarlet Rock Shrine. The real shrine is dedicated to two deities, [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]] and [[Toyotama-hime|Toyotamabime]]. The game's shrine is dedicated to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]].<ref name="vgc-2021a" />
==See also== *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Mihashira Torii]]
